title: Decide fate of schedule.day_of_month (surface it or remove it)
description:
The `schedule` table has a `day_of_month` column (packages/schema/src/schedule.ts)
that is consumed by the monthly traffic-light calculator
(packages/core/src/trafficLight/monthlyCalculator.ts) and the notification
scheduler infrastructure (app/src/infrastructure/expoNotificationScheduler.ts,
app/src/infrastructure/expoConsolidatedScheduler.ts), but it is NOT exposed
anywhere in the user-facing schedule editor
(app/src/components/HabitForm/ScheduleEntryForm.tsx et al). Users can only
create weekday-based schedules.

Per the project's design intent, nag is not a calendar — schedules are
day-of-week, not day-of-month — so day_of_month is effectively dead code
from the user's perspective.

## Decision needed

Pick one:

1. Remove day_of_month entirely: schema migration dropping the column,
   plus removing code paths in monthlyCalculator, the expo notification
   schedulers, queries, and command schemas. Simpler model, less latent
   surface area.
2. Keep and surface it: add UI in the schedule editor so users can
   actually create day-of-month schedules. Only worth doing if there's a
   real use case.

## Follow-up

docs/Model.md currently documents day_of_month as "present but not surfaced
in the UI" as a placeholder until this is resolved. Update that note once
a direction is chosen.
